name: Sweet Potato, Apple and Onion Gratin
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Sweet potatoes, peel and slice
3/4 lb Granny Smith apples, peeled and cored, thin sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Butter a 13X6 inch oval baking dish.
3. Slice sweet potatoes into 1/4-inch thick slices.
4. Melt butter in a heavy large skillet over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until tender, about 10 minutes.
5. Toss apples with flour in a medium bowl.
6. Arrange half of the sweet potato slices at the bottom of the prepared dish.
7. Top with half of the apple slices.
8. Arrange the remaining sweet potato slices and then the remaining apple slices.
9. Cover with sautéed onions.
10. Pour stock over all.
11. Bake until the sweet potatoes are tender, about 45 minutes. Maintain oven temperature.
12. Mix breadcrumbs, cheese, and crumbled bacon in a small bowl.
13. Sprinkle mixture over gratin.
14. Bake for an additional 20 minutes.
15. Preheat broiler. Broil until topping browns. (This has not been necessary at any time I have made this recipe as the oven browns the topping just fine for me).
16. Cool for 5 minutes and serve.
Substitution: Use Butternut or Acorn squash instead of the sweet potatoes.
